grader.method: fresh blind semantic read of every complete page dossier in canonical order, with independent rubric scoring and blocker review

wholePageReview.synopsis: The course home gives a coherent map from decision foundations through value, policy, model-based, imitation, safety, reproducibility, and research practice.
wholePageReview.feedback: The staged path, prerequisites, outcome language, and milestone projects accurately frame the released RL curriculum without overclaiming what browser exercises establish.
revisionPriorities: Keep the route-level summary synchronized if lesson prerequisites or capstone evidence contracts change.

wholePageReview.synopsis: This lesson distinguishes sequential decisions from isolated prediction by tracing how actions change later states, rewards, and options.
wholePageReview.feedback: The state-action-reward trajectory and return examples make delayed consequences observable, while the prediction and transfer checks require causal reasoning rather than definition recall.
revisionPriorities: Retain the explicit contrast between immediate reward and long-horizon return when examples are revised.

wholePageReview.synopsis: This lesson builds an MDP from states, actions, transitions, rewards, and discounting, then applies the model to concrete trajectories.
wholePageReview.feedback: Definitions, notation, and worked reward calculations agree; the treatment also separates the Markov modeling assumption from a claim that real observations are always sufficient.
revisionPriorities: Preserve the state-sufficiency caveat alongside any future MDP diagrams.

wholePageReview.synopsis: This lesson explains why observations can omit decision-relevant state and how histories or beliefs support action under partial observability.
wholePageReview.feedback: The hidden-state examples and changed-case checks expose perceptual aliasing cleanly, and the lesson is careful not to equate a belief state with direct access to the world state.
revisionPriorities: Keep uncertainty over hidden state visible whenever the belief-update example changes.

wholePageReview.synopsis: This lesson connects policies to the state-action frequencies they induce and shows why occupancy, not action labels alone, determines collected experience.
wholePageReview.feedback: The probability and visitation examples support the causal link from policy to data distribution, with appropriate boundaries around finite samples and environment dynamics.
revisionPriorities: Maintain the distinction between expected occupancy and one sampled trajectory.

wholePageReview.synopsis: The capstone integrates MDP specification, policy evaluation, control, and evidence reporting in a small tabular workbench.
wholePageReview.feedback: Its staged artifact, exact-versus-sampled comparisons, local checks, and rubric form a credible cumulative task while labeling deterministic fixtures and sampling evidence honestly.
revisionPriorities: Keep exact dynamic-programming results visually separated from sampled estimates in future revisions.

wholePageReview.synopsis: This lesson derives Bellman backups for known tabular dynamics and uses them to explain iterative policy evaluation and improvement.
wholePageReview.feedback: Inputs, backup transformation, and value outputs are explicit, the arithmetic trace is internally consistent, and the lesson states the known-model limitation.
revisionPriorities: Preserve the known-transition-model prerequisite near every runnable or simulated backup.

wholePageReview.synopsis: This lesson estimates values from complete sampled returns and distinguishes first-visit from every-visit Monte Carlo updates.
wholePageReview.feedback: The return traces, visit accounting, and unbiasedness discussion are technically careful: the guarantee is tied to the estimator and sampling assumptions rather than generalized to every finite dataset.
revisionPriorities: Keep the first-visit/every-visit sampling assumptions explicit if the estimator examples expand.

wholePageReview.synopsis: This lesson shows temporal-difference learning as a one-step bootstrap from observed reward and the next-state estimate.
wholePageReview.feedback: The target, error, and update are traced numerically, and termination is correctly distinguished from time-limit truncation so bootstrapping behavior remains causal and implementation-relevant.
revisionPriorities: Preserve the separate terminated and truncated flags in all future code variants.

wholePageReview.synopsis: This lesson contrasts on-policy SARSA with off-policy Q-learning through their different bootstrap actions and resulting risk sensitivity.
wholePageReview.feedback: Matched transitions make the target difference observable, while terminal masking, behavior-policy dependence, and exploration boundaries are handled accurately.
revisionPriorities: Keep both algorithms on the same transition when revising the comparison.

wholePageReview.synopsis: The capstone asks learners to compare Monte Carlo, TD, SARSA, and Q-learning under a controlled tabular protocol.
wholePageReview.feedback: The project stages, diagnostics, required evidence, and deterministic checks align with the value-method lessons and avoid treating a single run as a general performance conclusion.
revisionPriorities: Retain matched seeds and budgets as required comparison evidence.

wholePageReview.synopsis: This lesson replaces a value table with parameterized predictions and explains generalization, correlated updates, and instability.
wholePageReview.feedback: The feature-to-prediction-to-update mechanism is concrete and the worked gradient step is consistent; limitations of approximation are presented as consequences rather than vague warnings.
revisionPriorities: Keep the parameter-sharing consequence explicit in every worked update.

wholePageReview.synopsis: This lesson explains how replay buffers alter sample correlations and how delayed target networks stabilize bootstrap targets.
wholePageReview.feedback: The continuing, terminal, and time-limit targets are numerically consistent, and the delayed-versus-online target comparison accurately isolates the moving-target mechanism.
revisionPriorities: Preserve the 5.05, 1.0, and 5.05 mask contrast when refactoring the fixture.

wholePageReview.synopsis: This lesson assembles function approximation, replay, target networks, exploration, and clipped optimization into the DQN learning loop.
wholePageReview.feedback: The end-to-end data flow and tensor responsibilities are inspectable, while the evidence boundary correctly treats the browser experience as mechanism teaching rather than benchmark reproduction.
revisionPriorities: Keep tensor shapes and target detachment visible in code-facing explanations.

wholePageReview.synopsis: This lesson compares exploration choices in deep value learning and explains how coverage and uncertainty affect what can be learned.
wholePageReview.feedback: Controlled cases distinguish action randomness from useful state coverage and acknowledge that exploration bonuses or schedules do not guarantee discovery.
revisionPriorities: Retain a failure case where nominal exploration still misses important states.

wholePageReview.synopsis: The capstone integrates a DQN-style pipeline with explicit replay, target, exploration, evaluation, and failure-analysis artifacts.
wholePageReview.feedback: The scaffold and checkpoints demand inspectable evidence at each mechanism boundary, and the rubric avoids rewarding a high return without reproducibility or diagnostics.
revisionPriorities: Keep learning curves paired with replay and target-network diagnostics.

wholePageReview.synopsis: This lesson derives the likelihood-ratio policy-gradient estimator and connects sampled returns to increases or decreases in action log-probability.
wholePageReview.feedback: The sign and weighting of the gradient contribution are explained through concrete action probabilities, with sampling variance and on-policy data requirements stated clearly.
revisionPriorities: Preserve the explicit gradient-ascent sign convention beside the numerical trace.

wholePageReview.synopsis: This lesson uses baselines and generalized advantage estimation to trade variance against bias in policy-gradient updates.
wholePageReview.feedback: The delta recursion and advantage trace are numerically coherent, and the lesson distinguishes variance reduction from a change to the ideal expected policy gradient under valid baselines.
revisionPriorities: Keep the GAE boundary cases for lambda visible with the recursive calculation.

wholePageReview.synopsis: This lesson couples an actor update to a learned critic and traces how critic error shapes the policy signal.
wholePageReview.feedback: Actor and critic objectives, stop-gradient boundaries, and bootstrapped targets are separated precisely, including correct treatment of terminal versus truncated transitions.
revisionPriorities: Preserve distinct actor and critic loss labels in every implementation view.

wholePageReview.synopsis: The capstone integrates rollout collection, advantage estimation, actor-critic updates, and evaluation into an auditable on-policy experiment.
wholePageReview.feedback: It requires seed-aware evidence, diagnostics, and failure interpretation rather than a single score, and its local checks align with the preceding policy-gradient mechanisms.
revisionPriorities: Keep rollout freshness and policy-version provenance in the required artifact.

wholePageReview.synopsis: This lesson explains how a learned transition model can support control while introducing compounding prediction error and objective mismatch.
wholePageReview.feedback: The observation-action-to-next-state mechanism is worked concretely, and the lesson avoids assuming one-step prediction quality guarantees useful long-horizon plans.
revisionPriorities: Retain separate one-step and rollout-error diagnostics.

wholePageReview.synopsis: This lesson plans by sampling action sequences, predicting their returns, executing only the first action, and replanning.
wholePageReview.feedback: The shooting and receding-horizon trace is internally consistent and clearly shows why feedback from replanning can limit but not eliminate model error.
revisionPriorities: Keep the execute-one-action-then-replan step explicit in the worked trace.

wholePageReview.synopsis: This lesson combines real transitions with model-generated updates and examines how imagination changes sample use and error propagation.
wholePageReview.feedback: The real-versus-imagined update paths are distinguishable, and model bias is treated as a measurable risk rather than free extra data.
revisionPriorities: Preserve provenance labels for real and imagined transitions.

wholePageReview.synopsis: This lesson shows how a planner can exploit learned-model errors and how uncertainty-aware penalties or ensembles expose that failure.
wholePageReview.feedback: The matched candidate plans make exploitation causal, while the lesson correctly frames uncertainty as a warning signal rather than proof that a prediction is wrong.
revisionPriorities: Keep disagreement and true error conceptually separate in future ensemble examples.

wholePageReview.synopsis: The capstone integrates model learning, rollout validation, planning, uncertainty checks, and model-exploitation analysis.
wholePageReview.feedback: Its artifact contract and stop criteria require learners to inspect both task return and model failure, matching the evidence limits of model-based control.
revisionPriorities: Retain explicit plan-ranking reversals as a required exploitation diagnostic.

wholePageReview.synopsis: This lesson casts behavior cloning as supervised prediction on expert state-action pairs and explains its deployment distribution limit.
wholePageReview.feedback: The loss and probability update are traced correctly, and the changed-state example makes clear why low held-out imitation loss does not guarantee closed-loop control.
revisionPriorities: Keep offline imitation accuracy separate from rollout success.

wholePageReview.synopsis: This lesson explains covariate shift in sequential imitation and uses DAgger to query corrective actions on learner-visited states.
wholePageReview.feedback: The distribution feedback loop and aggregation cycle are concrete, with expert-query cost and safety limits included rather than hidden behind the algorithm.
revisionPriorities: Preserve the learner-state data provenance in each DAgger round.

wholePageReview.synopsis: This lesson explains why offline RL is constrained by dataset support and how out-of-distribution actions can receive unreliable values.
wholePageReview.feedback: The support and extrapolation examples are technically sound, and conservative action selection is framed as a response to evidence gaps rather than a universal optimality guarantee.
revisionPriorities: Keep action support visible beside every offline value estimate.

wholePageReview.synopsis: The capstone trains and audits a sequence-conditioned policy using shifted action targets, masks, context limits, and rollout evaluation.
wholePageReview.feedback: Token alignment and padding masks are causally explained, while required artifacts distinguish teacher-forced prediction from closed-loop policy evidence.
revisionPriorities: Retain the explicit input-target shift and masked-loss checks.

wholePageReview.synopsis: This lesson treats evaluation as estimation across seeds and episodes, using intervals and paired protocols to express uncertainty.
wholePageReview.feedback: The bootstrap fixture is reproducible and the reported interval of [-31.0, 4.6] matches the code contract; the narrative correctly resists conclusions unsupported by that interval.
revisionPriorities: Keep seed-level observations as the resampling unit when the fixture changes.

wholePageReview.synopsis: This lesson separates reward optimization from cost constraints and traces feasibility, trade-offs, and dual updates.
wholePageReview.feedback: The constrained objective and numerical cases are consistent, and the text makes clear that observed budget compliance does not by itself establish safety under distribution shift.
revisionPriorities: Preserve separate reward, cost, and constraint-violation reporting.

wholePageReview.synopsis: This lesson specifies reproducible RL runs through pinned configurations, seed control, logging, checkpoints, and budget-aware GPU execution.
wholePageReview.feedback: The short-run and full-run budgets are internally consistent at 200/137 and 20,000/19,937, and external execution is optional with explicit stop and evidence criteria.
revisionPriorities: Keep environment-step and update-count budgets recorded separately.

wholePageReview.synopsis: The final capstone asks learners to formulate, implement, evaluate, and report an RL research claim with reproducible evidence and limitations.
wholePageReview.feedback: Its staged protocol, local checks, failure logs, rubric, and machine-readable artifact integrate the full course while preventing a single favorable run from becoming a broad claim.
revisionPriorities: Retain the claim-to-evidence table and falsification criterion as required deliverables.
